articles.title: What to know about the MBTA’s free and discounted commuter rail plans for summer
articles.description:
The discounts are partly meant to offset impacts from commuter rail schedule changes during the World Cup in June and July.

The post What to know about the MBTA’s free and discounted commuter rail plans for summer appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: MBTA offers free and discounted commuter rail fares this summer.
Discounts aim to mitigate schedule changes during the World Cup in June and July.
Boston residents can benefit from reduced travel costs amid increased rail usage.

articles.title: Supreme Court seems poised to reject Trump’s birthright citizenship limits as he attends arguments
articles.description:
The justices heard Trump’s appeal of a lower-court ruling from New Hampshire that struck down the citizenship restrictions, one of several courts that have blocked them.

The post Supreme Court seems poised to reject Trump’s birthright citizenship limits as he attends arguments appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Supreme Court hears Trump's appeal on citizenship limits affecting Boston residents' rights.
New Hampshire court previously struck down Trump's restrictions, influencing national policy.
Potential rejection of limits could secure birthright citizenship for many Boston families.

articles.title: Weston native Tim Demirjian wins Hulu’s ‘Love Overboard’
articles.description:
The new reality dating show, streaming now, is executive produced by BU alum Alex “Call Her Daddy” Cooper.

The post Weston native Tim Demirjian wins Hulu’s ‘Love Overboard’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Weston native Tim Demirjian wins Hulu's reality dating show 'Love Overboard'.
The show is executive produced by Boston University alum Alex Cooper.
Demirjian's win highlights local talent in Boston's entertainment industry.

articles.title: American record-holder Conner Mantz withdraws from Boston Marathon
articles.description:
The last year’s top American and fourth-place finisher has been dealing with injury since the fall.

The post American record-holder Conner Mantz withdraws from Boston Marathon appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Conner Mantz, last year's top American finisher, withdraws from Boston Marathon due to injury.
Mantz finished fourth in the 2022 Boston Marathon, setting an American record.
His withdrawal may affect local race dynamics and competition among elite runners.

articles.title: MBTA to release train tickets for World Cup matches at Boston Stadium
articles.description:
Tickets will go on sale Wednesday, April 8.

The post MBTA to release train tickets for World Cup matches at Boston Stadium appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: MBTA will release train tickets for World Cup matches at Boston Stadium.
Tickets go on sale April 8, 2026, for local residents.
Increased public transport access expected for thousands attending matches.

articles.title: 2 Boston bars earn spots on 50 Best’s top 100 U.S. bars list
articles.description:
Both bars are new entries on the 50 Best list.

summary: Two Boston bars ranked in the top 100 U.S. bars by 50 Best.

This recognition may boost local tourism and bar patronage in Boston.

articles.title: Mass. man sentenced for online threats to public figures, children, elementary school
articles.description:
A 37-year-old man from Wilbraham received more than two and half years in prison for threatening social media posts.

The post Mass. man sentenced for online threats to public figures, children, elementary school appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Wilbraham man sentenced to 30 months for online threats against public figures and children.
The threats included alarming posts targeting an elementary school in Massachusetts.
The case highlights growing concerns over online safety for Boston area schools.

articles.title: The Liberty Hotel unveils a $75K World Cup ultra-luxe package
articles.description:
Guests will receive premium seats to a match, helicopter transportation, autographed jerseys, and more.

The post The Liberty Hotel unveils a $75K World Cup ultra-luxe package appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: The Liberty Hotel launches a $75K luxury World Cup package for affluent guests.
Package includes premium match seats, helicopter transport, and autographed jerseys.
Local economy may benefit from increased tourism and high-end spending during the World Cup.
